dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the {enemy_type} know we're coming for them. And they've responded the only way they know how.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They killed another one. A shepherd boy, barely twelve, found at the edge of the settlement this morning. {target_npc} is trying to keep people from forming a mob, but the rage is boiling over. If we don't {quest_action} now, the villagers will charge in themselves and get massacred. You need to hit the {enemy_type} lair {location_hint} before grief turns into a bloodbath.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every day we wait, {quest_threat} takes another life to prove they can. This has to end.

playerResponses.text: A child? No more waiting. I'm going now.
They're provoking us into a mistake. I'll stay focused and hit them where it hurts.
This cycle of killing will never stop. I can't be part of it.

dialogueChunks.intro: We found something at the {enemy_type} camp you raided, {player_name}. Something that changes everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Belongings. Personal items from at least a dozen victims, kept as trophies. {target_npc} identified their sister's pendant, and three other families found pieces of people they'd lost. {quest_threat} didn't just kill our people: they collected souvenirs. There's a deeper warren {location_hint} where the leader keeps their hoard. We need you to recover what you can as part of {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Those items belong to {quest_stakes}. They should never have been trophies.

playerResponses.text: Trophies from the dead? I'll bring everything back and make them pay.
Deeper into their territory for personal effects? The families need this. I'll go.
Going deeper into their lair alone is asking to join the dead. I won't do it.

dialogueChunks.plotStep: A second group has joined the ones you've been hunting: drawn by the commotion, or maybe called in. {target_npc} spotted them arriving {location_hint} in numbers we haven't seen before. They've doubled the guard and they're raiding more aggressively, gathering {quest_item} to fortify. If we don't break their alliance now as part of our efforts to {quest_action}, the settlement will face a force too large to overcome.
dialogueChunks.outro: Cut their supply line, scatter the reinforcements, and finish what we started for {quest_stakes}.

playerResponses.text: Reinforcements change nothing. I'll cut through them all.
Double the enemies means double the danger. I'll need more resources.
An enemy that's growing stronger while I fight alone? The odds are impossible.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need you to listen carefully. Some of our own people are wavering.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Half the settlement thinks we should negotiate with the {enemy_type} instead of fighting. {target_npc} is holding the community together, but barely. To prove that our fight to {quest_action} is worth finishing, we need a decisive strike. There's a forward outpost {location_hint} where {quest_threat} have been stockpiling {quest_item}. Destroy it, and we show {quest_stakes} that victory is within reach.
dialogueChunks.outro: If the settlement loses its nerve now, everyone who died will have died for nothing. We need this win.

playerResponses.text: A decisive strike to restore their courage. I'll make it count.
Internal divisions on top of external enemies? I'll handle the outpost, you handle the politics.
If your own people want peace, maybe you should listen to them.
